name: Game Hens in Chocolate Sauce
recipeInstructions:
1. Season the game hens with salt and pepper.
2. Heat olive oil in a large heavy pan and brown the birds on all sides.
3. Add garlic and stir until it takes on a pale color.
4. Add vinegar, 2 cups of chicken stock, vermouth, and onions to the pan. Bring to a boil.
5. Lower heat, cover tightly, and cook gently until the birds are very tender (about 1 hour).
6. Heat remaining chicken stock in a large enough pan to hold pear halves.
7. Add pear halves and poach until barely tender; remove with a slotted spoon and keep warm.
8. Add chestnuts to the poaching liquid and simmer until heated through; remove and keep warm.
9. Remove birds to a serving platter or plates.
10. Strain cooking liquid, return to heat, and add grated chocolate. Stir until it reaches a sauce consistency.
11. Pour over the birds and garnish with pears, chestnuts, fried bread, raisins, and parsley.
Note: To make fried bread, cut crusts from 4 slices of white bread, then cut slices diagonally to make 8 triangles. Fry quickly in hot oil until golden on both sides. Drain and keep warm.
